summary: Surah Al-Muddaththir calls the Prophet to arise and warn, magnify his Lord, purify, shun idols, avoid worldly favor-seeking, and be patient. It condemns the arrogant denier who called the Qur’an magic and human speech, warning of Saqar, Hell with nineteen angelic guards—a number that tests people of the Book, believers, and doubters. Every soul is pledged for its deeds; the people of the right question the guilty who admit neglecting prayer, not feeding the poor, indulging in vain talk, and denying the Day—until death overtook them. Reminder benefits only by Allah’s will.
